description: Ben Gummer served as Minister for the Cabinet Office and Paymaster General from July 2016 until June 2017. He was elected Conservative MP for Ipswich in 2010. Education Ben was educated at Tonbridge School in Kent, and studied History at Peterhouse, Cambridge. Political career Parliamentary Advisor to Lord Feldman from May 2012 to September 2012 Parliamentary Private Secretary to Minister of State for International Development Parliamentary Private Secretary to the Secretary of State for Education, October 2013 to May 2015 Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Quality at Department of Health from May 2015 to July 2016 Career outside politics After studying at Cambridge University, Ben ran a small engineering firm before becoming managing director of a consultancy business. He is also the author of a book on the Black Death - The Scourging Angel. Ben is an associate governor of Ravenswood Primary School, Patron of Home Start South Suffolk and a trustee of the Foundation Years Trust.

details.body:
Ben Gummer served as Minister for the Cabinet Office and Paymaster General from July 2016 until June 2017.

He was elected Conservative MP for Ipswich in 2010.

Education

Ben was educated at Tonbridge School in Kent, and studied History at Peterhouse, Cambridge.

Political career

  • Parliamentary Advisor to Lord Feldman from May 2012 to September 2012
  • Parliamentary Private Secretary to Minister of State for International Development
  • Parliamentary Private Secretary to the Secretary of State for Education, October 2013 to May 2015
  • Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Quality at Department of Health from May 2015 to July 2016

Career outside politics

After studying at Cambridge University, Ben ran a small engineering firm before becoming managing director of a consultancy business. He is also the author of a book on the Black Death - The Scourging Angel.

Ben is an associate governor of Ravenswood Primary School, Patron of Home Start South Suffolk and a trustee of the Foundation Years Trust.

  • Parliamentary Private Secretary to Minister of State for International Development
  • Parliamentary Private Secretary to the Secretary of State for Education, October 2013 to May 2015
  • Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Quality at Department of Health from May 2015 to July 2016

Career outside politics

After studying at Cambridge University, Ben ran a small engineering firm before becoming managing director of a consultancy business. He is also the author of a book on the Black Death - The Scourging Angel.

Ben is an associate governor of Ravenswood Primary School, Patron of Home Start South Suffolk and a trustee of the Foundation Years Trust.

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Ben Gummer - Paymaster General

details.body:
Ben Gummer served as Minister for the Cabinet Office and Paymaster General from July 2016 until June 2017.

He was elected Conservative MP for Ipswich in 2010.

Education

Ben was educated at Tonbridge School in Kent, and studied History at Peterhouse, Cambridge.

Political career

  • Parliamentary Advisor to Lord Feldman from May 2012 to September 2012
  • Parliamentary Private Secretary to Minister of State for International Development
  • Parliamentary Private Secretary to the Secretary of State for Education, October 2013 to May 2015
  • Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Quality at Department of Health from May 2015 to July 2016

Career outside politics

After studying at Cambridge University, Ben ran a small engineering firm before becoming managing director of a consultancy business. He is also the author of a book on the Black Death - The Scourging Angel.

Ben is an associate governor of Ravenswood Primary School, Patron of Home Start South Suffolk and a trustee of the Foundation Years Trust.

• Delivery of the government’s efficiency programme
  • Civil Service Modernisation and Reform
  • Places for growth programme
  • Cabinet Office business planning and performance
  • Infected Blood Inquiry 
  • Public bodies reform programme
  • Spend controls reform
  • Oversight of the cross-cutting functions and government functional strategy

Supporting the Deputy Prime Minister on:

  • Driving delivery of the government’s priorities 
  • Civil contingencies and resilience

role_appointments.title: The Rt Hon Ben Gummer - Minister for the Cabinet Office

details.body:
Ben Gummer served as Minister for the Cabinet Office and Paymaster General from July 2016 until June 2017.

He was elected Conservative MP for Ipswich in 2010.

Education

Ben was educated at Tonbridge School in Kent, and studied History at Peterhouse, Cambridge.

Political career

  • Parliamentary Advisor to Lord Feldman from May 2012 to September 2012
  • Parliamentary Private Secretary to Minister of State for International Development
  • Parliamentary Private Secretary to the Secretary of State for Education, October 2013 to May 2015
  • Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Quality at Department of Health from May 2015 to July 2016

Career outside politics

After studying at Cambridge University, Ben ran a small engineering firm before becoming managing director of a consultancy business. He is also the author of a book on the Black Death - The Scourging Angel.

Ben is an associate governor of Ravenswood Primary School, Patron of Home Start South Suffolk and a trustee of the Foundation Years Trust.

Ministerial responsibilities include:

  • end of life care
  • failing hospitals
  • hospital care
  • maternity care
  • pathology and death certification
  • patient experience, including
    • friends and family test
    • NHS Constitution
    • ombudsman and complaints
    • patient choice
    • Patient Reported Outcome Measures
  • patient safety
  • prison health services
  • secondary care commissioning policy
  • workforce, including:
    • education and training
    • Health Education England
    • nursing and midwifery
    • skills

ordered_parent_organisations.title: Department of Health and Social Care

role.title: Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for Care Quality
